name: Precious Metals Refinery
description_short: Phoenix-based precious metals buyer specializing in gold, jewelry, watches, and coins with free evaluations and in-home consultations for estates.
description_long:
Precious Metals Refinery (PMR) is a multi-location precious metals and jewelry buying business with locations in Phoenix and Tucson, Arizona, and Palm Desert and Palm Springs, California. The company operates as a buyer and refiner of precious metals rather than a traditional pawn shop, focusing on voluntary sales of valuables rather than collateral-based lending. PMR Phoenix operates from 4340 E Indian School Rd in Phoenix under manager Olga Cottone and is open Monday–Saturday, 10 AM–6 PM. The company has expanded into adjacent service lines including jewelry and watch repair, custom jewelry design, and estate consultation services.

PMR's core service is purchasing gold, silver, platinum, diamonds, coins, and luxury watches from consumers. They advertise free professional evaluations using industry-standard tools, conducted by GIA-certified gemologists, numismatists, and watch experts. The company offers in-home consultations for large estates or high-value collections and claims to provide competitive pricing with a stated guarantee to "beat ANY written offer" in Scottsdale, Tempe, and Phoenix. Secondary services include jewelry repair, watch servicing, and custom jewelry design. Transactions are conducted in private buying offices where customers remain present throughout the evaluation and sale process.

PMR differentiates itself through expertise credentials (GIA certification, specialist training), transparency practices (customers present during evaluation), and service breadth (repair and custom design capabilities alongside buying). The company emphasizes secure, confidential transactions and positions itself as a local alternative to national chains. Marketing materials stress honesty and fair pricing as core value propositions. The company also publishes educational content on topics like inherited jewelry and precious metals comparison.

As a precious metals buyer, PMR is fundamentally a liquidation service for consumers converting tangible assets to cash. The business model depends on acquiring undervalued items and reselling at retail or refinery rates. While the company claims competitive pricing and professional standards, consumers should independently verify valuations, particularly for high-value items like fine jewelry or luxury watches. The guarantee to beat competitor offers is conditional on written quotes and specific to three Arizona cities. Like all precious metals buyers, PMR's appeal and reliability depend heavily on local reputation and individual transaction experience.
address: 8140 S Houghton Rd Suite 100, Tucson, AZ 85747

company_info.headquarters: 8140 S Houghton Rd Suite 100, Tucson, AZ 85747

pros: GIA-certified gemologists and specialist staff (numismatists, watch experts) provide credentialed evaluations
Free professional evaluations with no pressure to sell
Customers remain present and unencumbered during evaluation and transaction process
In-home consultation service available for large estates and high-value collections
Expanded service offerings including jewelry/watch repair and custom design reduce need for external vendors
Price-match guarantee to beat written competitor offers in Phoenix, Scottsdale, and Tempe
Multi-location network (4 locations across Arizona and California) provides geographic convenience
cons: No independent verification available; claims of 'top dollar' and 'competitive rates' cannot be objectively validated from website alone
Price-match guarantee is limited to written offers in three specific Arizona cities, excluding broader comparison
Business model inherently depends on purchasing items below market value, creating built-in misalignment with seller interests
No information on how pricing is calculated, what markup is applied, or how refinery rates are determined
Luxury watch and designer jewelry specialization may create higher-pressure sales environment for inexperienced sellers of high-value items
best_for: Estate executors liquidating inherited jewelry, watches, or coin collections quickly
Owners of luxury watches (Rolex, Cartier, Omega, Patek Philippe) seeking specialist evaluation and immediate cash
Consumers with large precious metals collections or designer jewelry requiring in-home evaluation for security
diagnosis: PMR is best suited for estate liquidators, luxury watch owners, and consumers with significant precious metals holdings who want credentialed specialist evaluation and local service convenience. The critical caveat is that as a precious metals buyer, the company's financial incentive is to acquire items below retail/refinery value; consumers should obtain independent appraisals or competitive quotes before accepting any offer, particularly for high-value items.
services: Luxury watch buying and authentication (Rolex, Cartier, Omega, Breitling, Audemars Piguet, Patek Philippe)
Designer jewelry buying (Tiffany & Co., David Yurman, Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels)
In-home consultations for estates and bulk collections
